sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
LEFT didn't change the board. The player is blocked. Looking at the image, the player is at the top left of the maze. The white corridor at col 12 is to the left of the player. But the player is blocked by black walls.
Let me re-examine the maze structure. Looking at the image:
- The player (blue square) is at the top left of the maze.
- There's a white square below and to the right of the player.
- The white vertical corridor is at the left edge of the maze.
I think the player needs to go DOWN to enter the white corridor. Let me check the area below the player.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check player position and area around
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
blue_player = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] == 24]
p = blue_player[0]['boundary']
r_min, r_max = p[0][0], p[2][0]
c_min, c_max = p[0][1], p[2][1]

ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print(f"Player at rows {r_min}-{r_max}, cols {c_min}-{c_max}")
print(f"\nArea around player (rows {r_min-2}-{r_max+5}, cols {c_min-3}-{c_max+3}):")
for i in range(max(0, r_min-2), min(63, r_max+6)):
    print(f"{i:2d}: {ascii_lines[i][max(0,c_min-3):min(64,c_max+3)]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
